name: async-mutex never deletes lock entries (dead cleanup branch)
description: [FCR 2026-06-21 · L1-04] locks.get(key) === current is always false (map stores a new then-promise, not current), so delete never runs; one bounded leaked entry per key. Fix: store/compare the same promise object.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: apps/mobile/src/lib/async-mutex.ts stores a chained promise in locks but compares locks.get(key) to the earlier current promise in finally, so the cleanup branch never deletes the map entry. Variants: single keyed lock cleanup path; cover both sequential reuse after a completed lock and concurrent queued lock ordering so cleanup does not break mutual exclusion. Regression test: apps/mobile/src/lib/async-mutex.test.ts fails without the fix and passes with it, asserting the lock entry is released after completion while existing ordering tests still pass.
